assertions.expectation: Strongly advises AGAINST moving to 'No channel' — it provides LESS control, not more
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'No, moving to "No channel" is not recommended and will actually reduce your upgrade control, not increase it' and calls it a 'common misconception'

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' only supports the basic 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion, while release channels support all three exclusion types including 'no minor or node upgrades' (up to EoS)
assertions.reason: Response includes a comparison table showing 'No channel' only has 'Only "no upgrades" exclusions' with '30 days max' while release channels have all exclusion types including 'No minor or node upgrades' exclusion

assertions.expectation: Recommends staying on Regular channel (or moving to Stable/Extended) and using maintenance exclusions for control
assertions.reason: Response has section 'Maximum control configuration (stay on Regular)' and also mentions Extended channel as an alternative, with specific gcloud commands for Regular channel configuration

assertions.expectation: Specifically recommends the 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion as the way to prevent disruptive changes while allowing security patches
assertions.reason: Response shows gcloud command with '--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades' and explains 'Security patches auto-applied to control plane only' while preventing 'Zero minor version or node upgrades'

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Comparison table shows Extended support (24 months) as 'Yes' for Release Channels and 'No' for No Channel, and Extended channel section mentions 'Up to 24 months of support per version'

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration and the industry is moving away from it
assertions.reason: Response describes 'No channel' as 'a legacy configuration' in the section explaining why it's a trap

assertions.expectation: Mentions that maintenance windows control WHEN upgrades happen, while exclusions control WHAT upgrades happen — together they give fine-grained control
assertions.reason: While the response shows maintenance window configuration and exclusions working together, it does not explicitly explain the distinction between WHEN (maintenance windows) vs WHAT (exclusions) upgrades happen

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risks of 'No channel': no granular exclusions, no Extended support, risk of snowflaking clusters
assertions.reason: Response covers no granular exclusions and no Extended support, but does not mention the risk of snowflaking clusters as a specific concern with 'No channel'
